violations.text: The door to the outside, located in the hallway (next to ice machine), is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2026
The hood system did not have a filter installed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2026

Raw animal foods (chicken) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (cheese) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2026

Food was observed held at 44-50 degrees Fahrenheit in prep unit #2. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been transferred from the walk-in cooler to the prep unit about 2 hours prior to inspection. Food was relocated back to the prep unit #1 at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2026

Dressing and coleslaw which was prepared on site, date marked 12/28 and 1/03 was observed held beyond the 7 days. Food items exceeding the 7 days shall be discarded. See disposal. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2026

Chlorine sanitizer solution at the dishwasher was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC called for service at time of inspection. Establishment to not use dishwasher until it is dispensing proper concentration. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2026

The door to the outside, located at the hallway (next to the ice machine), is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2026
Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package in the basement dry storage area. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2026

Prep unit #2 had an ambient temperature of 48 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Prep unit was taken out of service and must not be used until it can maintain a temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2026
Missing ceiling tiles were observed in the kitchen and basement dry storage area. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2026

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2026
The hood system did not have a filter installed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2026

Food was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2025

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2025

Fish was observed thawing in the prep sink. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2025

Reduced oxygen (ROP) seafood (red snapper) was observed stored with the package intact. ROP seafood must be removed from the package when thawing. The ROP red snapper was observed held at 36 degrees Fahrenheit in the walk-in cooler at time of inspection. ROP red snapper was removed from the package at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2025

The ice machine, prep sink, and ice well are lacking an air gap. A direct connection must not exist between the sewage system and a drain originating from equipment in which food is placed. New Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Please email inspector proof of course completion. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/27/2023
The ceiling tiles in the basement area and above the dishwasher are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/27/2023
The condenser fan in the walk-in cooler basement area is in poor repair. Condenser fan is leaking. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/27/2023

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Please enroll additional employees in an approved food safety course. New Violation. Correct By: 09/27/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Ice machine was taken out of service and must not be used until it is cleaned. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/27/2023

Food was observed held at 44-60 degrees Fahrenheit in prep unit #1. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been transferred from the walk-in cooler to the prep unit about 2 hours prior to inspection. Food was relocated back to the walk-in cooler during time of inspection. The prep unit had an ambient air temperature of 44 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Prep unit was taken out of service and must not be used until it can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/13/2023
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods (seared ahi tuna) on the menu that are raw or undercooked. New Violation. Correct By: 09/27/2023

Reduced oxygen packaged (ROP) seafood (tuna) was observed thawing with the package intact. ROP seafood must be removed from the package or the package must be cut open when thawing. Tuna was observed held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Tuna was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/27/2023

The presence of flies and rodents are not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings and flies in the basement dry storage area near the onions. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Pest control services must be contacted. New Violation. Correct By: 09/27/2023
Onions were observed stored on the floor in the basement. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/27/2023

The ceilings in the basement area and above the dishwasher are in poor repair. Ceiling tiles are missing. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/27/2023

The walk-in cooler in the basement area is in poor repair. Condenser fan is leaking and the door is not tight fitting. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/27/2023
